# Test Plan
Generated by /plan-eng-review on 2026-03-18
Branch: main
Repo: Local-Roots

## Affected Pages/Routes

### Backend Endpoints (new)
- `POST /v1/internal/reengagement` вҖ” trigger lapsed subscriber email scan
- `GET /v1/cities` вҖ” list cities with active stores and slugs
- `POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/box-updates` вҖ” create "what's in the box" content
- `GET /v1/stores/{storeId}/box-updates` вҖ” public: list box updates for a store
- `POST /v1/waitlist` вҖ” add email to waitlist with geo coordinates
- `POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/locations` вҖ” add pickup location
- `PUT /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/locations/{locationId}` вҖ” update location
- `DELETE /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/locations/{locationId}` вҖ” remove location

### Backend Endpoints (modified)
- `GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/analytics` вҖ” new fields: retention_rate, avg_rating, review_count, subscriber_trend, revenue_growth_pct, top_products, no_show_rate
- `transferToSeller()` вҖ” now records errors and retry count

### Frontend Pages (new)
- `/farms/{city}` вҖ” city landing page with stores in that area
- `/sitemap.xml` вҖ” dynamic sitemap
- `/robots.txt` вҖ” robots file
- `/stores/{storeId}/opengraph-image` вҖ” dynamic OG image

### Frontend Pages (modified)
- `/seller/stores/{storeId}/analytics` вҖ” new metric cards, retention, trends, top products
- `/seller/stores/{storeId}/settings` вҖ” "Pickup Locations" management section
- `/stores/{storeId}` вҖ” JSON-LD structured data, box updates display
- `/stores` вҖ” waitlist form on empty search results

## Key Interactions to Verify
- Seller posts "what's in the box" content on dashboard вҶ’ appears on buyer-facing store page
- Buyer searches for farms, finds nothing вҶ’ sees email capture form вҶ’ submits вҶ’ sees "you're on the list"
- Buyer with lapsed subscription вҶ’ receives re-engagement email (check inbox after 6h cron)
- Buyer picks up box вҶ’ receives review prompt email ~2 hours later
- Buyer hits 5th pickup вҶ’ receives milestone celebration email
- Seller views analytics page вҶ’ sees retention rate, avg rating, subscriber trend, top products
- Seller receives Monday digest email with weekly metrics summary
- Transfer fails вҶ’ shows "Processing" вҶ’ retry succeeds вҶ’ shows "Transferred"
- Transfer fails 3x вҶ’ shows "Action needed" on payout history
- Seller adds second pickup location вҶ’ buyer sees location picker on subscribe form
- Store page shared on social media вҶ’ shows branded OG image with store name + price

## Edge Cases
- Re-engagement email: subscriber pauses between query and send (should be skipped)
- Re-engagement email: subscriber who was nudged, picked up once, then lapsed again (14-day cooldown)
- Milestone email: subscriber cancels and re-subscribes (count cumulative pickups)
- Analytics: brand new store with zero data (all metrics show empty/zero state)
- Transfer retry: order was refunded after capture but before transfer (skip, don't retry)
- City landing page: city name with spaces ("San Francisco" вҶ’ slug "san-francisco")
- Waitlist notification: store goes live but in a different city than waitlist entry (match by radius, not city name)
- OG image: store has no hero image and no reviews (render branded fallback)
- Location delete: location has future pickup windows (block delete, show error)
- Box update: seller posts then deletes (should clean up, not leave orphan)

## Critical Paths
- Full re-engagement flow: active sub misses 2 cycles вҶ’ cron detects вҶ’ email sent вҶ’ timestamp updated вҶ’ not re-sent within 14 days
- Full transfer retry flow: pickup confirmed вҶ’ transfer fails вҶ’ error recorded вҶ’ retry cron picks up вҶ’ retries with same idempotency key вҶ’ succeeds вҶ’ transfer_id saved
- Full milestone flow: buyer's 10th pickup вҶ’ cron detects вҶ’ checks milestone_emails table вҶ’ not yet sent вҶ’ sends email вҶ’ records in milestone_emails вҶ’ next run skips
- Full waitlist flow: buyer signs up on empty search вҶ’ store goes live in area вҶ’ cron matches by radius вҶ’ notification sent вҶ’ buyer subscribes

## Section 4: Performance Review

| Concern | Severity | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| **Re-engagement NOT EXISTS query** | Med | Add index `orders(subscription_id, status, picked_up_at)` вҖ” noted in architecture fixes |
| **Subscriber trend 12-week CTE** | Low | Single CTE with `generate_series`. At <100 subs/store, sub-millisecond. Add comment noting this should migrate to snapshots at 1000+ subs |
| **Analytics endpoint: 4+ additional queries** | Med | Combine review aggregation + retention + no-show rate into a single CTE. Keep top products separate (different table). Reduces round trips from 4 to 2 |
| **OG image generation** | Low | Add `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600` header. Images only change when store data changes |
| **City aggregation** | Low | Simple GROUP BY on a small dataset. Fine at current scale |
| **Sitemap generation** | Low | Queries all stores + cities. Fine at <100 stores. Add ISR/caching note for 10K+ |

**No decisions needed.** All performance items are implementation notes вҖ” combine queries, add indexes, add cache headers. Moving on.

---

## Failure Modes

| Codepath | Failure Mode | Test? | Error Handling? | User Impact | Critical? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| RunReengagement | DB connection pool exhausted | Y | Y (log + exit) | Silent | No |
| RunReengagement | Resend API rate limit | Y | Y (stop batch) | Silent | No |
| RunTransferRetry | Stripe charge already refunded | Y | Y (skip + record) | "Action needed" | No |
| RunTransferRetry | Stripe idempotency key collision | N | Y (Stripe handles) | Silent | No |
| RunMilestoneCheck | Two cron runs detect same milestone | Y | Y (unique constraint on milestone_emails) | Silent | No |
| RunWaitlistNotify | Waitlist email bounces | N | Y (Resend handles) | Silent | No |
| RunSellerDigest | Seller has no email (shouldn't happen) | Y | Y (skip) | Silent | No |
| City landing page | City slug collision (two "Springfield"s) | N | N | **Wrong results** | **WARNING** |
| OG image | ImageResponse timeout (large images) | N | Y (Next.js default) | Fallback | No |

**1 WARNING:** City slug collision. Two cities named "Springfield" in different states would collide at `/farms/springfield`. Resolution: use `city-region` as the slug (e.g., `springfield-il`, `springfield-mo`). **Obvious fix вҖ” noted for implementation.**

**0 CRITICAL GAPS.** All failure modes have either test coverage or framework-level error handling.

| Photo upload | Supabase integration | Yes вҖ” reuse for box update photos |
| Transfer logic | transferToSeller() | Yes вҖ” extend with error recording |

### TODOS.md updates

### Diagrams needed in code

| File | Diagram Type | Content |

`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`
**Retention rate vs. active rate:** This is a common analytics trap. "What % of my subscribers are active?" (a snapshot) is very different from "What % of subscribers who were active last month are still active?" (a retention rate). The first can look great while hiding massive churn вҖ” if you're acquiring new subscribers fast enough, the percentage stays high even as old ones leave. The lifecycle-event approximation (`1 - churn_rate`) gives you the real signal. This distinction matters enormously for product-market fit evaluation.

**City slug collision:** The `springfield` problem is a real-world version of the "namespace collision" pattern. Any time you derive a unique identifier from user-provided data (city names, usernames, file names), assume collisions exist. The `city-region` slug pattern (`springfield-il`) is the standard solution вҖ” same approach GitHub uses for `owner/repo`.
`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`

## Preamble (run first)

```bash
_UPD=$(~/.claude/skills/gstack/bin/gstack-update-check 2>/dev/null || .claude/skills/gstack/bin/gstack-update-check 2>/dev/null || true)

_CONTRIB=$(~/.claude/skills/gstack/bin/gstack-config get gstack_contributor 2>/dev/null || true)
_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
echo "BRANCH: $_BRANCH"
_LAKE_SEEN=$([ -f ~/.gstack/.completeness-intro-seen ] && echo "yes" || echo "no")

```

If output shows `UPGRADE_AVAILABLE <old> <new>`: read `~/.claude/skills/gstack/gstack-upgrade/SKILL.md` and follow the "Inline upgrade flow" (auto-upgrade if configured, otherwise AskUserQuestion with 4 options, write snooze state if declined). If `JUST_UPGRADED <from> <to>`: tell user "Running gstack v{to} (just updated!)" and continue.

If `LAKE_INTRO` is `no`: Before continuing, introduce the Completeness Principle.
Tell the user: "gstack follows the **Boil the Lake** principle вҖ” always do the complete
thing when AI makes the marginal cost near-zero. Read more: https://garryslist.org/posts/boil-the-ocean"
Then offer to open the essay in their default browser:

```bash
open https://garryslist.org/posts/boil-the-ocean
touch ~/.gstack/.completeness-intro-seen
```

Only run `open` if the user says yes. Always run `touch` to mark as seen. This only happens once.

## AskUserQuestion Format

**ALWAYS follow this structure for every AskUserQuestion call:**
1. **Re-ground:** State the project, the current branch (use the `_BRANCH` value printed by the preamble вҖ” NOT any branch from conversation history or gitStatus), and the current plan/task. (1-2 sentences)
2. **Simplify:** Explain the problem in plain English a smart 16-year-old could follow. No raw function names, no internal jargon, no implementation details. Use concrete examples and analogies. Say what it DOES, not what it's called.
3. **Recommend:** `RECOMMENDATION: Choose [X] because [one-line reason]` вҖ” always prefer the complete option over shortcuts (see Completeness Principle). Include `Completeness: X/10` for each option. Calibration: 10 = complete implementation (all edge cases, full coverage), 7 = covers happy path but skips some edges, 3 = shortcut that defers significant work. If both options are 8+, pick the higher; if one is вүӨ5, flag it.
4. **Options:** Lettered options: `A) ... B) ... C) ...` вҖ” when an option involves effort, show both scales: `(human: ~X / CC: ~Y)`

Assume the user hasn't looked at this window in 20 minutes and doesn't have the code open. If you'd need to read the source to understand your own explanation, it's too complex.

Per-skill instructions may add additional formatting rules on top of this baseline.

## Completeness Principle вҖ” Boil the Lake

AI-assisted coding makes the marginal cost of completeness near-zero. When you present options:

- If Option A is the complete implementation (full parity, all edge cases, 100% coverage) and Option B is a shortcut that saves modest effort вҖ” **always recommend A**. The delta between 80 lines and 150 lines is meaningless with CC+gstack. "Good enough" is the wrong instinct when "complete" costs minutes more.
- **Lake vs. ocean:** A "lake" is boilable вҖ” 100% test coverage for a module, full feature implementation, handling all edge cases, complete error paths. An "ocean" is not вҖ” rewriting an entire system from scratch, adding features to dependencies you don't control, multi-quarter platform migrations. Recommend boiling lakes. Flag oceans as out of scope.
- **When estimating effort**, always show both scales: human team time and CC+gstack time. The compression ratio varies by task type вҖ” use this reference:

| Task type | Human team | CC+gstack | Compression |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| Boilerplate / scaffolding | 2 days | 15 min | ~100x |
| Test writing | 1 day | 15 min | ~50x |
| Feature implementation | 1 week | 30 min | ~30x |
| Bug fix + regression test | 4 hours | 15 min | ~20x |
| Architecture / design | 2 days | 4 hours | ~5x |
| Research / exploration | 1 day | 3 hours | ~3x |

- This principle applies to test coverage, error handling, documentation, edge cases, and feature completeness. Don't skip the last 10% to "save time" вҖ” with AI, that 10% costs seconds.

**Anti-patterns вҖ” DON'T do this:**
- BAD: "Choose B вҖ” it covers 90% of the value with less code." (If A is only 70 lines more, choose A.)
- BAD: "We can skip edge case handling to save time." (Edge case handling costs minutes with CC.)
- BAD: "Let's defer test coverage to a follow-up PR." (Tests are the cheapest lake to boil.)
- BAD: Quoting only human-team effort: "This would take 2 weeks." (Say: "2 weeks human / ~1 hour CC.")

## Contributor Mode

If `_CONTRIB` is `true`: you are in **contributor mode**. You're a gstack user who also helps make it better.

**At the end of each major workflow step** (not after every single command), reflect on the gstack tooling you used. Rate your experience 0 to 10. If it wasn't a 10, think about why. If there is an obvious, actionable bug OR an insightful, interesting thing that could have been done better by gstack code or skill markdown вҖ” file a field report. Maybe our contributor will help make us better!

**Calibration вҖ” this is the bar:** For example, `$B js "await fetch(...)"` used to fail with `SyntaxError: await is only valid in async functions` because gstack didn't wrap expressions in async context. Small, but the input was reasonable and gstack should have handled it вҖ” that's the kind of thing worth filing. Things less consequential than this, ignore.

**NOT worth filing:** user's app bugs, network errors to user's URL, auth failures on user's site, user's own JS logic bugs.

**To file:** write `~/.gstack/contributor-logs/{slug}.md` with **all sections below** (do not truncate вҖ” include every section through the Date/Version footer):

```
# {Title}

Hey gstack team вҖ” ran into this while using /{skill-name}:

**What I was trying to do:** {what the user/agent was attempting}
**What happened instead:** {what actually happened}
**My rating:** {0-10} вҖ” {one sentence on why it wasn't a 10}

## Steps to reproduce
1. {step}

## Raw output
```
{paste the actual error or unexpected output here}
```

## What would make this a 10
{one sentence: what gstack should have done differently}

**Date:** {YYYY-MM-DD} | **Version:** {gstack version} | **Skill:** /{skill}
```

Slug: lowercase, hyphens, max 60 chars (e.g. `browse-js-no-await`). Skip if file already exists. Max 3 reports per session. File inline and continue вҖ” don't stop the workflow. Tell user: "Filed gstack field report: {title}"

## Step 0: Detect base branch

Determine which branch this PR targets. Use the result as "the base branch" in all subsequent steps.

1. Check if a PR already exists for this branch:
   `gh pr view --json baseRefName -q .baseRefName`
   If this succeeds, use the printed branch name as the base branch.

2. If no PR exists (command fails), detect the repo's default branch:
   `gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef -q .defaultBranchRef.name`

3. If both commands fail, fall back to `main`.

Print the detected base branch name. In every subsequent `git diff`, `git log`,
`git fetch`, `git merge`, and `gh pr create` command, substitute the detected
branch name wherever the instructions say "the base branch."

---

# /plan-design-review: Designer's Eye Plan Review

You are a senior product designer reviewing a PLAN вҖ” not a live site. Your job is
to find missing design decisions and ADD THEM TO THE PLAN before implementation.

The output of this skill is a better plan, not a document about the plan.

## Design Philosophy

You are not here to rubber-stamp this plan's UI. You are here to ensure that when
this ships, users feel the design is intentional вҖ” not generated, not accidental,
not "we'll polish it later." Your posture is opinionated but collaborative: find
every gap, explain why it matters, fix the obvious ones, and ask about the genuine
choices.

Do NOT make any code changes. Do NOT start implementation. Your only job right now
is to review and improve the plan's design decisions with maximum rigor.

## Design Principles

1. Empty states are features. "No items found." is not a design. Every empty state needs warmth, a primary action, and context.
2. Every screen has a hierarchy. What does the user see first, second, third? If everything competes, nothing wins.
3. Specificity over vibes. "Clean, modern UI" is not a design decision. Name the font, the spacing scale, the interaction pattern.
4. Edge cases are user experiences. 47-char names, zero results, error states, first-time vs power user вҖ” these are features, not afterthoughts.
5. AI slop is the enemy. Generic card grids, hero sections, 3-column features вҖ” if it looks like every other AI-generated site, it fails.
6. Responsive is not "stacked on mobile." Each viewport gets intentional design.
7. Accessibility is not optional. Keyboard nav, screen readers, contrast, touch targets вҖ” specify them in the plan or they won't exist.
8. Subtraction default. If a UI element doesn't earn its pixels, cut it. Feature bloat kills products faster than missing features.
9. Trust is earned at the pixel level. Every interface decision either builds or erodes user trust.

## Cognitive Patterns вҖ” How Great Designers See

These aren't a checklist вҖ” they're how you see. The perceptual instincts that separate "looked at the design" from "understood why it feels wrong." Let them run automatically as you review.

1. **Seeing the system, not the screen** вҖ” Never evaluate in isolation; what comes before, after, and when things break.
2. **Empathy as simulation** вҖ” Not "I feel for the user" but running mental simulations: bad signal, one hand free, boss watching, first time vs. 1000th time.
3. **Hierarchy as service** вҖ” Every decision answers "what should the user see first, second, third?" Respecting their time, not prettifying pixels.
4. **Constraint worship** вҖ” Limitations force clarity. "If I can only show 3 things, which 3 matter most?"
5. **The question reflex** вҖ” First instinct is questions, not opinions. "Who is this for? What did they try before this?"
6. **Edge case paranoia** вҖ” What if the name is 47 chars? Zero results? Network fails? Colorblind? RTL language?
7. **The "Would I notice?" test** вҖ” Invisible = perfect. The highest compliment is not noticing the design.
8. **Principled taste** вҖ” "This feels wrong" is traceable to a broken principle. Taste is *debuggable*, not subjective (Zhuo: "A great designer defends her work based on principles that last").
9. **Subtraction default** вҖ” "As little design as possible" (Rams). "Subtract the obvious, add the meaningful" (Maeda).
10. **Time-horizon design** вҖ” First 5 seconds (visceral), 5 minutes (behavioral), 5-year relationship (reflective) вҖ” design for all three simultaneously (Norman, Emotional Design).
11. **Design for trust** вҖ” Every design decision either builds or erodes trust. Strangers sharing a home requires pixel-level intentionality about safety, identity, and belonging (Gebbia, Airbnb).
12. **Storyboard the journey** вҖ” Before touching pixels, storyboard the full emotional arc of the user's experience. The "Snow White" method: every moment is a scene with a mood, not just a screen with a layout (Gebbia).

Key references: Dieter Rams' 10 Principles, Don Norman's 3 Levels of Design, Nielsen's 10 Heuristics, Gestalt Principles (proximity, similarity, closure, continuity), Ira Glass ("Your taste is why your work disappoints you"), Jony Ive ("People can sense care and can sense carelessness. Different and new is relatively easy. Doing something that's genuinely better is very hard."), Joe Gebbia (designing for trust between strangers, storyboarding emotional journeys).

When reviewing a plan, empathy as simulation runs automatically. When rating, principled taste makes your judgment debuggable вҖ” never say "this feels off" without tracing it to a broken principle. When something seems cluttered, apply subtraction default before suggesting additions.

## Priority Hierarchy Under Context Pressure

Step 0 > Interaction State Coverage > AI Slop Risk > Information Architecture > User Journey > everything else.
Never skip Step 0, interaction states, or AI slop assessment. These are the highest-leverage design dimensions.

## PRE-REVIEW SYSTEM AUDIT (before Step 0)

Before reviewing the plan, gather context:

```bash
git log --oneline -15
git diff <base> --stat
```

Then read:
- The plan file (current plan or branch diff)
- CLAUDE.md вҖ” project conventions
- DESIGN.md вҖ” if it exists, ALL design decisions calibrate against it
- TODOS.md вҖ” any design-related TODOs this plan touches

Map:
* What is the UI scope of this plan? (pages, components, interactions)
* Does a DESIGN.md exist? If not, flag as a gap.
* Are there existing design patterns in the codebase to align with?
* What prior design reviews exist? (check reviews.jsonl)

### Retrospective Check
Check git log for prior design review cycles. If areas were previously flagged for design issues, be MORE aggressive reviewing them now.

### UI Scope Detection
Analyze the plan. If it involves NONE of: new UI screens/pages, changes to existing UI, user-facing interactions, frontend framework changes, or design system changes вҖ” tell the user "This plan has no UI scope. A design review isn't applicable." and exit early. Don't force design review on a backend change.

Report findings before proceeding to Step 0.

## Step 0: Design Scope Assessment

### 0A. Initial Design Rating
Rate the plan's overall design completeness 0-10.
- "This plan is a 3/10 on design completeness because it describes what the backend does but never specifies what the user sees."
- "This plan is a 7/10 вҖ” good interaction descriptions but missing empty states, error states, and responsive behavior."

Explain what a 10 looks like for THIS plan.

### 0B. DESIGN.md Status
- If DESIGN.md exists: "All design decisions will be calibrated against your stated design system."
- If no DESIGN.md: "No design system found. Recommend running /design-consultation first. Proceeding with universal design principles."

### 0C. Existing Design Leverage
What existing UI patterns, components, or design decisions in the codebase should this plan reuse? Don't reinvent what already works.

### 0D. Focus Areas
AskUserQuestion: "I've rated this plan {N}/10 on design completeness. The biggest gaps are {X, Y, Z}. Want me to review all 7 dimensions, or focus on specific areas?"

**STOP.** Do NOT proceed until user responds.

## The 0-10 Rating Method

For each design section, rate the plan 0-10 on that dimension. If it's not a 10, explain WHAT would make it a 10 вҖ” then do the work to get it there.

Pattern:
1. Rate: "Information Architecture: 4/10"
2. Gap: "It's a 4 because the plan doesn't define content hierarchy. A 10 would have clear primary/secondary/tertiary for every screen."
3. Fix: Edit the plan to add what's missing
4. Re-rate: "Now 8/10 вҖ” still missing mobile nav hierarchy"
5. AskUserQuestion if there's a genuine design choice to resolve
6. Fix again вҶ’ repeat until 10 or user says "good enough, move on"

Re-run loop: invoke /plan-design-review again вҶ’ re-rate вҶ’ sections at 8+ get a quick pass, sections below 8 get full treatment.

## Review Sections (7 passes, after scope is agreed)

### Pass 1: Information Architecture
Rate 0-10: Does the plan define what the user sees first, second, third?
FIX TO 10: Add information hierarchy to the plan. Include ASCII diagram of screen/page structure and navigation flow. Apply "constraint worship" вҖ” if you can only show 3 things, which 3?
**STOP.** AskUserQuestion once per issue. Do NOT batch. Recommend + WHY. If no issues, say so and move on. Do NOT proceed until user responds.

### Pass 2: Interaction State Coverage
Rate 0-10: Does the plan specify loading, empty, error, success, partial states?
FIX TO 10: Add interaction state table to the plan:
```
  FEATURE              | LOADING | EMPTY | ERROR | SUCCESS | PARTIAL
  ---------------------|---------|-------|-------|---------|--------
  [each UI feature]    | [spec]  | [spec]| [spec]| [spec]  | [spec]
```
For each state: describe what the user SEES, not backend behavior.
Empty states are features вҖ” specify warmth, primary action, context.
**STOP.** AskUserQuestion once per issue. Do NOT batch. Recommend + WHY.

### Pass 3: User Journey & Emotional Arc
Rate 0-10: Does the plan consider the user's emotional experience?
FIX TO 10: Add user journey storyboard:
```
  STEP | USER DOES        | USER FEELS      | PLAN SPECIFIES?
  -----|------------------|-----------------|----------------
  1    | Lands on page    | [what emotion?] | [what supports it?]
  ...
```
Apply time-horizon design: 5-sec visceral, 5-min behavioral, 5-year reflective.
**STOP.** AskUserQuestion once per issue. Do NOT batch. Recommend + WHY.

### Pass 4: AI Slop Risk
Rate 0-10: Does the plan describe specific, intentional UI вҖ” or generic patterns?
FIX TO 10: Rewrite vague UI descriptions with specific alternatives.
- "Cards with icons" вҶ’ what differentiates these from every SaaS template?
- "Hero section" вҶ’ what makes this hero feel like THIS product?
- "Clean, modern UI" вҶ’ meaningless. Replace with actual design decisions.
- "Dashboard with widgets" вҶ’ what makes this NOT every other dashboard?
**STOP.** AskUserQuestion once per issue. Do NOT batch. Recommend + WHY.

### Pass 5: Design System Alignment
Rate 0-10: Does the plan align with DESIGN.md?
FIX TO 10: If DESIGN.md exists, annotate with specific tokens/components. If no DESIGN.md, flag the gap and recommend `/design-consultation`.
Flag any new component вҖ” does it fit the existing vocabulary?
**STOP.** AskUserQuestion once per issue. Do NOT batch. Recommend + WHY.

### Pass 6: Responsive & Accessibility
Rate 0-10: Does the plan specify mobile/tablet, keyboard nav, screen readers?
FIX TO 10: Add responsive specs per viewport вҖ” not "stacked on mobile" but intentional layout changes. Add a11y: keyboard nav patterns, ARIA landmarks, touch target sizes (44px min), color contrast requirements.
**STOP.** AskUserQuestion once per issue. Do NOT batch. Recommend + WHY.

### Pass 7: Unresolved Design Decisions
Surface ambiguities that will haunt implementation:
```
  DECISION NEEDED              | IF DEFERRED, WHAT HAPPENS
  -----------------------------|---------------------------
  What does empty state look like? | Engineer ships "No items found."
  Mobile nav pattern?          | Desktop nav hides behind hamburger
  ...
```
Each decision = one AskUserQuestion with recommendation + WHY + alternatives. Edit the plan with each decision as it's made.

## CRITICAL RULE вҖ” How to ask questions
Follow the AskUserQuestion format from the Preamble above. Additional rules for plan design reviews:
* **One issue = one AskUserQuestion call.** Never combine multiple issues into one question.
* Describe the design gap concretely вҖ” what's missing, what the user will experience if it's not specified.
* Present 2-3 options. For each: effort to specify now, risk if deferred.
* **Map to Design Principles above.** One sentence connecting your recommendation to a specific principle.
* Label with issue NUMBER + option LETTER (e.g., "3A", "3B").
* **Escape hatch:** If a section has no issues, say so and move on. If a gap has an obvious fix, state what you'll add and move on вҖ” don't waste a question on it. Only use AskUserQuestion when there is a genuine design choice with meaningful tradeoffs.

## Required Outputs

### "NOT in scope" section
Design decisions considered and explicitly deferred, with one-line rationale each.

### "What already exists" section
Existing DESIGN.md, UI patterns, and components that the plan should reuse.

### TODOS.md updates
After all review passes are complete, present each potential TODO as its own individual AskUserQuestion. Never batch TODOs вҖ” one per question. Never silently skip this step.

For design debt: missing a11y, unresolved responsive behavior, deferred empty states. Each TODO gets:
* **What:** One-line description of the work.
* **Why:** The concrete problem it solves or value it unlocks.
* **Pros:** What you gain by doing this work.
* **Cons:** Cost, complexity, or risks of doing it.
* **Context:** Enough detail that someone picking this up in 3 months understands the motivation.
* **Depends on / blocked by:** Any prerequisites.

Then present options: **A)** Add to TODOS.md **B)** Skip вҖ” not valuable enough **C)** Build it now in this PR instead of deferring.

### Completion Summary
```
  +====================================================================+
  |         DESIGN PLAN REVIEW вҖ” COMPLETION SUMMARY                    |
  +====================================================================+
  | System Audit         | [DESIGN.md status, UI scope]                |
  | Step 0               | [initial rating, focus areas]               |
  | Pass 1  (Info Arch)  | ___/10 вҶ’ ___/10 after fixes                |
  | Pass 2  (States)     | ___/10 вҶ’ ___/10 after fixes                |
  | Pass 3  (Journey)    | ___/10 вҶ’ ___/10 after fixes                |
  | Pass 4  (AI Slop)    | ___/10 вҶ’ ___/10 after fixes                |
  | Pass 5  (Design Sys) | ___/10 вҶ’ ___/10 after fixes                |
  | Pass 6  (Responsive) | ___/10 вҶ’ ___/10 after fixes                |
  | Pass 7  (Decisions)  | ___ resolved, ___ deferred                 |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------------+
  | NOT in scope         | written (___ items)                         |
  | What already exists  | written                                     |
  | TODOS.md updates     | ___ items proposed                          |
  | Decisions made       | ___ added to plan                           |
  | Decisions deferred   | ___ (listed below)                          |
  | Overall design score | ___/10 вҶ’ ___/10                             |
  +====================================================================+
```

If all passes 8+: "Plan is design-complete. Run /design-review after implementation for visual QA."
If any below 8: note what's unresolved and why (user chose to defer).

### Unresolved Decisions
If any AskUserQuestion goes unanswered, note it here. Never silently default to an option.

## Review Log

After producing the Completion Summary above, persist the review result:

```bash
eval $(~/.claude/skills/gstack/bin/gstack-slug 2>/dev/null)
mkdir -p ~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG
echo '{"skill":"plan-design-review","timestamp":"TIMESTAMP","status":"STATUS","overall_score":N,"unresolved":N,"decisions_made":N}' >> ~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/$BRANCH-reviews.jsonl
```

Substitute values from the Completion Summary:
- **TIMESTAMP**: current ISO 8601 datetime
- **STATUS**: "clean" if overall score 8+ AND 0 unresolved; otherwise "issues_open"
- **overall_score**: final overall design score (0-10)
- **unresolved**: number of unresolved design decisions
- **decisions_made**: number of design decisions added to the plan

## Review Readiness Dashboard

After completing the review, read the review log and config to display the dashboard.

```bash
eval $(~/.claude/skills/gstack/bin/gstack-slug 2>/dev/null)
cat ~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/$BRANCH-reviews.jsonl 2>/dev/null || echo "NO_REVIEWS"
echo "---CONFIG---"
~/.claude/skills/gstack/bin/gstack-config get skip_eng_review 2>/dev/null || echo "false"
```

Parse the output. Find the most recent entry for each skill (plan-ceo-review, plan-eng-review, plan-design-review, design-review-lite). Ignore entries with timestamps older than 7 days. For Design Review, show whichever is more recent between `plan-design-review` (full visual audit) and `design-review-lite` (code-level check). Append "(FULL)" or "(LITE)" to the status to distinguish. Display:

```
+====================================================================+
|                    REVIEW READINESS DASHBOARD                       |
+====================================================================+
| Review          | Runs | Last Run            | Status    | Required |
|-----------------|------|---------------------|-----------|----------|
| Eng Review      |  1   | 2026-03-16 15:00    | CLEAR     | YES      |
| CEO Review      |  0   | вҖ”                   | вҖ”         | no       |
| Design Review   |  0   | вҖ”                   | вҖ”         | no       |
+--------------------------------------------------------------------+
| VERDICT: CLEARED вҖ” Eng Review passed                                |
+====================================================================+
```

**Review tiers:**
- **Eng Review (required by default):** The only review that gates shipping. Covers architecture, code quality, tests, performance. Can be disabled globally with \`gstack-config set skip_eng_review true\` (the "don't bother me" setting).
- **CEO Review (optional):** Use your judgment. Recommend it for big product/business changes, new user-facing features, or scope decisions. Skip for bug fixes, refactors, infra, and cleanup.
- **Design Review (optional):** Use your judgment. Recommend it for UI/UX changes. Skip for backend-only, infra, or prompt-only changes.

**Verdict logic:**
- **CLEARED**: Eng Review has >= 1 entry within 7 days with status "clean" (or \`skip_eng_review\` is \`true\`)
- **NOT CLEARED**: Eng Review missing, stale (>7 days), or has open issues
- CEO and Design reviews are shown for context but never block shipping
- If \`skip_eng_review\` config is \`true\`, Eng Review shows "SKIPPED (global)" and verdict is CLEARED

## Formatting Rules
* NUMBER issues (1, 2, 3...) and LETTERS for options (A, B, C...).
* Label with NUMBER + LETTER (e.g., "3A", "3B").
* One sentence max per option.
* After each pass, pause and wait for feedback.
* Rate before and after each pass for scannability.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user invoked `/plan-ceo-review` to review what they were last working on in the Local Roots project (a local pickup marketplace). After investigation, it was determined that Phase 8 (Growth & Retention) from the roadmap was the next priority. The user chose option B: draft a detailed Phase 8 implementation plan first, then CEO-review it. After the CEO review passed, the user invoked `/plan-eng-review` which also passed. The user then invoked `/plan-design-review` to review the UI-facing aspects of the Phase 8 plan before implementation.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Local Roots**: A local pickup marketplace with Go backend + Next.js App Router frontend + PostgreSQL
   - **Phase 8 scope**: Buyer re-engagement, enhanced seller analytics, SEO/public profiles, payout hardening, multi-location UX
   - **6 CEO-accepted expansions**: "What's in the box" preview, email capture on empty search (waitlist), seller weekly digest email, subscriber milestone celebrations, dynamic OG images, post-pickup review prompt
   - **Architecture decisions resolved**: `box_updates` table for weekly content, `milestone_emails` table for tracking, lifecycle-event-based retention rate (not snapshots), CTE with generate_series for subscriber trend, city-region slugs, 14:00 UTC Monday for seller digest
   - **Design system**: CSS variables (`--lr-bg`, `--lr-ink`, `--lr-muted`, `--lr-leaf`, `--lr-clay`, `--lr-water`), component classes (`lr-card`, `lr-btn`, `lr-chip`), warm earthy aesthetic
   - **Existing infrastructure**: Resend email client with 11 templates, in-process cron scheduler (2 jobs вҶ’ will become 8), Stripe Connect with auto-transfers on pickup/no-show, haversine geo search, Supabase photo uploads
   - **gstack review workflow**: CEO review вҶ’ Eng review вҶ’ Design review вҶ’ implementation
   - **Boil the Lake principle**: Always do the complete implementation when AI makes marginal cost near-zero

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `docs/plans/2026-03-18-phase-8-growth-retention.md` вҖ” **CREATED**: The full Phase 8 implementation plan with 5 tasks (re-engagement, analytics, SEO, payout hardening, multi-location). Contains SQL queries, Go struct definitions, TypeScript examples, migration specs, and verification checklists. This is the primary plan being reviewed.
   
   - `TODOS.md` вҖ” **CREATED**: First TODOS.md in the project, containing one deferred item:
     ```markdown
     ## P2: "Next pickup" date badge on store cards
     **What:** Show "Next pickup: Sat Mar 22" on each store card in `/stores` browse results and city landing pages.
     **Effort:** S (human: ~4h / CC: ~10 min)
     ```
   
   - `~/.gstack/projects/paretoimproved-local-roots/ceo-plans/2026-03-18-phase-8-growth-retention.md` вҖ” **CREATED**: CEO plan document with scope decisions, vision, and architecture decisions. Status: ACTIVE.
   
   - `~/.gstack/projects/paretoimproved-local-roots/brandonqueener-main-test-plan-20260318-065542.md` вҖ” **CREATED**: Test plan artifact for `/qa` consumption with affected routes, key interactions, edge cases, and critical paths.
   
   - `~/.gstack/projects/paretoimproved-local-roots/main-reviews.jsonl` вҖ” **CREATED**: Review log with two entries (CEO review clean, Eng review clean).
   
   - `docs/roadmap.md` вҖ” **READ**: Shows Phases 1-7 complete, Phase 8 "Growth + retention" is next with: Stripe Connect payouts/transfer automation, seller dashboard analytics, buyer re-engagement, multi-location support, public store profiles/SEO.
   
   - `docs/strategy/02-product-strategy.md` вҖ” **READ**: Full product strategy with MoSCoW prioritization, personas, differentiators, phased roadmap, KPIs. Key insight: 60%+ monthly subscription renewal is the #1 PMF signal. Zero seller fees is core differentiator.
   
   - `docs/strategy/04-ceo-strategy-memo.md` вҖ” **READ**: CEO strategy document with unit economics (net $1.64/transaction at 7%+$0.35), break-even at ~610 farmers, fundraising milestones, risk analysis, moat analysis.
   
   - `docs/domain.md` вҖ” **READ**: Domain model with order state machine (`placed вҶ’ ready вҶ’ picked_up`, `placed вҶ’ canceled`, `ready вҶ’ no_show`).
   
   - `docs/audits/growth-lead-audit.md` вҖ” **READ**: 30+ conversion recommendations. Key items relevant to Phase 8: empty state email capture (B4), "what's in the box" content (B19), post-pickup review prompt (B29), SEO infrastructure gaps (L3).
   
   - `docs/plans/2026-03-07-seller-dashboard-redesign.md` вҖ” **READ**: Previous plan (already implemented) for seller dashboard Pickups/Manage tabs with global pickup code entry. Shows the plan format and implementation style.
   
   - `frontend/src/app/globals.css` вҖ” **READ**: Design tokens:
     ```css
     --lr-bg: #f6f1e8;        /* sand */
     --lr-ink: #1c1b16;       /* ink */
     --lr-muted: #4a463c;     /* peat */
     --lr-card: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.78);
     --lr-card-strong: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.92);
     --lr-border: rgba(38, 28, 10, 0.12);
     --lr-leaf: #2f6b4f;      /* sage */
     --lr-clay: #b35d2e;      /* clay */
     --lr-water: #1f6c78;     /* river */
     ```
   
   - `frontend/src/app/seller/stores/[storeId]/analytics/page.tsx` вҖ” **READ**: Current analytics page with MetricCard component pattern (xs uppercase label, 2xl bold value, xs muted sub). Has a leftover `TODO(human)` for formatPickupRate function. Uses `lr-card` class pattern.
   
   - `frontend/src/app/stores/page.tsx` вҖ” **READ**: Stores browse page with Google Places autocomplete, radius search, debounced input. Already has `notifyEmail` and `notifySubmitted` state variables (partial waitlist implementation may exist).
   
   - `frontend/src/components/store-card.tsx` вҖ” **READ**: StoreCard component with image/placeholder, compact variant, "New" badge, distance chip, hover lift effect (`hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-[0_22px_60px_rgba(38,28,10,0.14)]`).

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **User rejected gstack project file reads and memory reads early in conversation**: The user denied tool use for reading `~/.gstack/projects/` and `~/.claude/projects/.../memory/MEMORY.md`. No explicit feedback given вҖ” I adjusted by not attempting those reads again and proceeded with git/docs investigation instead.
   - **No other errors encountered**: The reviews proceeded smoothly through all phases.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Finding what to review**: The user initially asked to review "what we were last working on." Investigation revealed the latest Entire.io checkpoints were just favicon work. The substantive prior work (seller dashboard redesign) was already completed. Determined Phase 8 from the roadmap was the logical next focus.
   - **Retention rate query bug**: The plan's retention rate SQL was mathematically wrong (snapshot vs. true retention). Resolved by using lifecycle event approximation: `1 - (canceled_in_30d / (active + canceled_in_30d))`.

content.text:
Good. Here are the **PRE-REVIEW SYSTEM AUDIT** findings:

---

## PRE-REVIEW SYSTEM AUDIT

**UI Scope:** This plan has **significant UI scope** вҖ” 4 new pages/files (`/farms/{city}`, `sitemap.ts`, `robots.ts`, OG image route), 3 modified pages (seller analytics, seller settings, store detail), 1 modified component (subscribe-form for location picker), plus a new waitlist form on the stores browse page and "what's in the box" display on the store detail page.

**DESIGN.md Status:** No DESIGN.md exists. Design tokens are defined inline in `globals.css`. Recommend running `/design-consultation` to formalize the design system.

**Existing Design Patterns to Reuse:**
- **MetricCard component** (`analytics/page.tsx:15-38`) вҖ” xs uppercase label, 2xl semibold value, xs muted sub
- **StoreCard component** (`store-card.tsx`) вҖ” image/placeholder, hover lift effect, distance chip, "New" badge
- **lr-card/lr-card-strong** вҖ” glassmorphic card with white/78% and white/92% opacity
- **lr-btn/lr-btn-primary** вҖ” primary CTA buttons (leaf green)
- **lr-chip** вҖ” rounded pill badges
- **lr-field** вҖ” form inputs
- **Color tokens** вҖ” `--lr-bg` (sand), `--lr-ink` (ink), `--lr-muted` (peat), `--lr-leaf` (sage), `--lr-clay` (clay), `--lr-water` (river)
- **Typography hierarchy** вҖ” 3xl for page headings, base for section headings, xs uppercase for labels, sm for body text
- **Payout status chips** вҖ” leaf green background for "Transferred", clay for "Pending" (`analytics/page.tsx:219-229`)
- **Empty states** вҖ” stores browse already has a warm empty state with email capture (`stores/page.tsx:372-408`)
- **Subscribe form** вҖ” fee breakdown pattern (subtotal / service fee / total), checkout-then-authorize flow

**Prior Review Cycles:** CEO review (clean) and Eng review (clean) both passed on 2026-03-18. No prior design reviews exist for any branch вҖ” this is the first.

**Leftover issue:** There's a `TODO(human)` at `analytics/page.tsx:41` for `formatPickupRate`. This should be resolved during Phase 8 analytics enhancement.

---

## Step 0: Design Scope Assessment

### 0A. Initial Design Rating

**This plan is a 4/10 on design completeness.**

It describes backend data flows and SQL queries in detail, but the frontend specs are vague:
- "Show metric cards row" (which metrics get emphasis? what's the visual hierarchy?)
- "Boxes from $XX/wk" (where on the card? what typography weight?)
- "Small ranked list" for top products (list? table? horizontal bars?)
- No empty states specified for any new feature (analytics with zero data, box updates when none posted, city page with no stores)
- No loading states specified
- No error states specified
- No responsive behavior mentioned anywhere
- No interaction states for new forms (waitlist, box updates posting)
- The "What's in the box" display has no design spec at all вҖ” just "appears on buyer-facing store page"
- The waitlist form already exists partially in code (`stores/page.tsx:42-43`) but the plan doesn't acknowledge this

**What a 10 looks like for this plan:** Every new UI element has a defined visual hierarchy (what the user sees first/second/third), interaction state coverage (loading/empty/error/success), responsive behavior per viewport, specific typography and spacing using existing design tokens, and emotional arc for key user journeys (buyer discovering a waitlist, seller seeing analytics growth, buyer reading "what's in the box").

### 0B. DESIGN.md Status
No design system found. All design decisions will be calibrated against universal design principles and the implicit patterns in `globals.css` + existing components. **Recommend running `/design-consultation` after Phase 8 to formalize what's becoming a mature design language.**

### 0C. Existing Design Leverage
The plan should reuse these existing patterns:
1. **MetricCard** component вҖ” already built, just needs new instances for retention, avg rating, etc.
2. **Empty state with email capture** вҖ” the stores browse page already has the waitlist empty state UI. The plan should note this and extend it (add geo coords, connect to backend) rather than building from scratch.
3. **Payout status chips** вҖ” extend the existing green/amber pattern for the new transfer statuses.
4. **Store detail page layout** вҖ” the "What's in the box" content should follow the existing section pattern (h2 heading + content cards).
5. **lr-card + lr-card-strong** вҖ” all new content sections should use the established card hierarchy.
### 0D. Focus Areas

## Pass 1: Information Architecture

**Rating: 3/10**

The plan introduces 6 new UI surfaces but doesn't define what the user sees first, second, third on any of them. Here's what's missing and what I'll add:

**1a. Seller Analytics Page вҖ” Enhanced Metrics**

The plan says "metric cards row" with retention, avg rating, subscriber trend, revenue growth, top products, no-show rate. That's 6 new metrics added to the existing 4 (active subs, pickup rate, revenue, churn) = **10 total metric cards**. Ten cards in a flat grid is visual soup вҖ” nothing has hierarchy.

в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ           в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 2. Small Fruit Box     28 sold   $840                в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 3. ...                                               в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Revenue by Cycle (existing table)                                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Payout History (existing table, enhanced statuses)                 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

Key design decisions:
- **Primary row** = the 4 metrics sellers check daily (subs, pickup rate, revenue, rating). Avg Rating replaces Churn in the top row вҖ” it's more emotionally salient.
- **Secondary row** = operational health metrics (retention, no-show, churn). Smaller cards, muted presentation.
- **Top Products** = a ranked list section, not a card grid. Horizontal bar chart would be slick but a simple numbered list is more readable and consistent with the existing table patterns.
- **Subscriber Trend** = rendered as a small trend arrow (в–І/в–ј + delta) on the Active Subscribers card, not a separate sparkline. Keeps it scannable.

**1b. Store Detail Page вҖ” "What's in the Box"**

The plan says this "appears on buyer-facing store page" with zero layout spec. Proposed hierarchy:

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  [What's available вҖ” subscription plans + walk-up]                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  [Reviews]                                                          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

Key decisions:
- Placed **above** "What's available" вҖ” it builds anticipation before the subscribe CTA (per growth audit: #1 subscriber question is "what's in the box?")
- Single card with optional photo on the left, title + body on the right
- "Posted X days ago" timestamp gives freshness signal
- If no box update exists, section doesn't render (not an empty state вҖ” it's optional content)

```

Minimal page вҖ” h1 with city/region, one-line subtitle for SEO, then the store grid using existing `StoreCard`. No new components needed.

**1d. Waitlist Form on Empty Search**

This already partially exists in code (`stores/page.tsx:372-408`). The plan should note: **enhance existing UI**, don't rebuild. Just add:
- Geo coords submission (lat/lng from current search context)
- Backend API call instead of just `setNotifySubmitted(true)` (currently a no-op)

No information architecture change needed вҖ” the existing layout is good.

questions.question: For the seller analytics page, I'm proposing a two-tier metric card layout: 4 primary cards (Active Subs with trend arrow, Pickup Rate, Revenue with growth %, Avg Rating with stars) and 3 secondary cards below (Retention Rate, No-Show Rate, Churn). This replaces the plan's flat 'add all metrics as cards' approach. The existing Churn card moves from primary to secondary row since retention rate is a more actionable metric for sellers. Does this hierarchy feel right for what sellers care about most?

content.text:
Two-tier layout locked in. I'll add that to the plan. No other info architecture questions вҖ” the city landing page and "what's in the box" placement are straightforward uses of existing patterns.

**Pass 1 re-rated: 3/10 вҶ’ 7/10** (hierarchy defined for all surfaces, but not yet reflected in the plan file вҖ” will update at the end).

---

## Pass 2: Interaction State Coverage

**Rating: 1/10** вҖ” The plan specifies zero interaction states for any new feature.

Here's the complete interaction state table I'll add to the plan:

```
FEATURE                      | LOADING              | EMPTY                    | ERROR                | SUCCESS              | PARTIAL
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
Seller analytics (enhanced)  | Existing skeleton    | "Your store is brand     | Existing toast       | Data renders in      | Some metrics null
                             | "Loading analyticsвҖҰ" | new вҖ” metrics appear     | pattern              | two-tier cards       | (avg_rating null вҶ’
                             |                      | after your first pickup" |                      |                      | show "No reviews yet")
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
"What's in the box" display  | N/A (server-rendered | Section doesn't render   | N/A (server-side     | Card with photo,     | Photo missing вҶ’
(buyer store page)           | with page)           | вҖ” not an empty state     | fetch, graceful      | title, body,         | text-only card
                             |                      |                          | degradation)         | timestamp            |
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
Box update posting (seller)  | "PostingвҖҰ" disabled  | "Share what's in this    | ErrorAlert with      | Toast "Update        | Photo upload fails
                             | button               | week's box with your     | retry prompt         | posted!" + card      | вҶ’ post text-only,
                             |                      | subscribers"             |                      | appears in list      | show warning
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
Waitlist email capture       | "JoiningвҖҰ" disabled  | N/A (only shows when     | "Couldn't join       | "You're on the       | Already on list вҶ’
                             | button               | search has 0 results)    | waitlist" inline     | list!" green check   | "You're already
                             |                      |                          | error                |                      | on our list"
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
City landing page            | Skeleton cards       | "No farms in {city} yet. | "Couldn't load       | Store grid renders   | N/A
(/farms/{city})              | (3 placeholders)     | Leave your emailвҖҰ"       | farms" with retry    |                      |
                             |                      | (reuse waitlist form)    |                      |                      |
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
Top products list            | Inline with page     | "No orders yet вҖ”         | N/A (part of         | Ranked list with     | N/A
                             | load                 | products appear after    | analytics load)      | quantity + revenue   |
                             |                      | your first pickup"       |                      |                      |
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
Payout status (enhanced)     | Existing table load  | Existing "no payouts"    | Existing pattern     | 4-state chips:       | N/A
                             |                      |                          |                      | Transferred (green), |
                             |                      |                          |                      | Processing (amber),  |
                             |                      |                          |                      | Retrying (red),      |
                             |                      |                          |                      | Action needed (red)  |
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
Multi-location management    | "SavingвҖҰ" on forms   | "Add your first pickup   | ErrorAlert on save   | Toast "Location      | Delete blocked вҶ’
(seller settings)            |                      | location" with CTA       | failure              | saved/removed"       | "Can't remove вҖ”
                             |                      |                          |                      |                      | active pickups exist"
-----------------------------|----------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------------
Location picker (buyer       | N/A (static render)  | N/A (only shows when     | N/A                  | Selected location    | Single location вҶ’
subscribe form)              |                      | >1 location exists)      |                      | highlighted          | no picker shown
```

Key design decisions in this table:

1. **Analytics empty state** вҖ” "Your store is brand new вҖ” metrics appear after your first pickup" is warm and specific. Not "No data found."
2. **Box updates** вҖ” if none exist, the section simply doesn't render on the buyer page. It's opt-in content, not a feature with an empty state. But the *seller* posting UI needs a warm prompt.
3. **Payout status** вҖ” 4-state chip system using existing color tokens: `--lr-leaf` (green/transferred), amber (processing), `--lr-clay` (red/retrying + action needed). Distinguish "Retrying" from "Action needed" by whether retry limit is exceeded.
4. **City empty state** вҖ” reuses the waitlist form pattern already built on `/stores`. Same warm copy, same email capture.
5. **Nullable metrics** вҖ” `avg_rating` can be null for stores with zero reviews. Show "No reviews yet" with a subtle link to "Ask your subscribers to leave a review" rather than showing "0.0 stars".

questions.question: For the seller's 'What's in the Box' posting UI: when a seller hasn't posted any box updates yet, should the empty state be (A) a standalone section in settings with a warm prompt and CTA, or (B) integrated inline on the seller dashboard page near their upcoming pickup window? Option B puts it closer to the action вҖ” the seller is already looking at their next pickup when they'd think 'I should tell subscribers what's coming.' Option A is more discoverable but separated from context.

options.label: Dashboard inline (Recommended)
options.description: Show 'Share what's in this week's box' prompt near the upcoming pickup on the seller dashboard. Contextually relevant вҖ” seller sees it when managing their next pickup.

content.text:
Dashboard inline it is вҖ” contextually placed near the next pickup. I'll add this to the plan.

**Pass 2 re-rated: 1/10 вҶ’ 8/10** (all states covered, warm empty states specified, partial/error states defined).

---

## Pass 3: User Journey & Emotional Arc

**Rating: 3/10** вҖ” The plan describes data flows, not experiences.

Three key user journeys in this plan:

**3a. Buyer discovers "What's in the Box"**
```
STEP | USER DOES              | USER FEELS           | PLAN SPECIFIES?
-----|------------------------|----------------------|------------------
1    | Lands on store page    | Curious, browsing    | Yes (existing hero)
2    | Sees box update card   | Anticipation builds  | NOW YES (card spec)
3    | Reads contents list    | "Oh, strawberries!"  | NOW YES (body text)
4    | Scrolls to subscribe   | Ready to commit      | Yes (existing CTA)
```
The emotional arc is: **curiosity вҶ’ anticipation вҶ’ desire вҶ’ action**. The box update is the hinge вҖ” it converts "I should subscribe someday" into "I want THIS box."

**3b. Seller sees analytics growth**
```
STEP | USER DOES              | USER FEELS           | PLAN SPECIFIES?
-----|------------------------|----------------------|------------------
1    | Opens analytics        | "How am I doing?"    | NOW YES (two-tier)
2    | Sees subscriber в–І +3   | Pride, momentum      | NOW YES (trend arrow)
3    | Sees 4.8вҳ… avg rating   | Validation           | NOW YES (stars)
4    | Checks top products    | Strategic insight     | NOW YES (ranked list)
5    | Sees retention 78%     | Reassurance           | NOW YES (secondary)
```
Arc: **curiosity вҶ’ pride вҶ’ validation вҶ’ insight**. The two-tier layout serves this вҖ” hero metrics first (emotional), operational metrics second (rational).

**3c. Buyer hits empty search вҶ’ waitlist**
```
STEP | USER DOES              | USER FEELS           | PLAN SPECIFIES?
-----|------------------------|----------------------|------------------
1    | Searches for farms     | Hopeful              | Yes (existing search)
2    | Sees "No farms"        | Disappointed         | Partially (existing)
3    | Reads warm copy        | "Oh, they care"      | NOW YES (warm copy)
4    | Enters email           | "At least I'll know" | NOW YES (form + API)
5    | Sees confirmation      | Relieved, patient    | NOW YES (success msg)
```
Arc: **hope вҶ’ disappointment вҶ’ surprise (warmth) вҶ’ trust**. The warm copy at step 3 is critical вҖ” it turns a dead end into a relationship.

No design decision needed here вҖ” the journeys confirm the hierarchy and state decisions already made. Moving on.

**Pass 3 re-rated: 3/10 вҶ’ 8/10**

---

## Pass 4: AI Slop Risk

**Rating: 6/10** вҖ” Most of the plan reuses existing patterns (good), but a few areas risk feeling generic.

**4a. City landing page** вҖ” The plan says "server-rendered pages showing farms in a city." Every AI-generated marketplace has a generic `/category/{slug}` page with a grid of cards. What makes this feel like Local Roots?

The existing StoreCard already has the brand's warm aesthetic вҖ” reusing it is the right call. The risk is the page header. "Farm Boxes in Austin, TX" is fine for SEO, but the subtitle should feel warm, not template-y.

Fix: Instead of a generic "Browse local farms" subtitle, use: "Fresh local food, picked up weekly from farms near you." This is specific to what Local Roots does and matches the product's voice.

**4b. Analytics dashboard** вҖ” "Dashboard with metric cards" is the quintessential AI slop pattern. The two-tier layout helps, but the real differentiation is in the *content*. The MetricCard component already has the right aesthetic (xs uppercase labels, warm ink colors). The key is making the new metrics feel *useful*, not just *present*:
- Subscriber trend as в–І/в–ј with delta (not a separate sparkline chart)
- Avg Rating as filled/unfilled stars (not just a number)
- Top Products as a simple ranked list (not a chart.js bar graph)

These are intentional restraint choices вҖ” they feel hand-crafted, not "we installed a dashboard library."

**4c. OG image** вҖ” The plan says "branded social share card." Risk: generic gradient with white text. The plan should specify: use `--lr-bg` (sand) background, `--lr-ink` (ink) text, `--lr-leaf` (sage) accent. Store name, price, and review stars. No stock photos, no gradients.

No questions needed вҖ” these are obvious fixes I'll add to the plan.

**Pass 4 re-rated: 6/10 вҶ’ 8/10**

---

## Pass 5: Design System Alignment

**Rating: 5/10** вҖ” No DESIGN.md exists, but the plan should explicitly reference the design tokens it will use.

The implicit design system from `globals.css` and existing components:

| Token | Value | Usage in Phase 8 |
|-------|-------|-------------------|
| `--lr-bg` | `#f6f1e8` (sand) | Page backgrounds, OG image background |
| `--lr-ink` | `#1c1b16` (ink) | Primary text, metric values |
| `--lr-muted` | `#4a463c` (peat) | Labels, secondary text, timestamps |
| `--lr-leaf` | `#2f6b4f` (sage) | Positive indicators (transferred, trend в–І), CTAs |
| `--lr-clay` | `#b35d2e` (clay) | Warning/error indicators (retrying, action needed) |
| `--lr-water` | `#1f6c78` (river) | Currently unused вҖ” could be used for informational states |
| `lr-card` | white/78% | Standard cards |
| `lr-card-strong` | white/92% | Emphasized cards (primary metrics, forms) |
| `lr-chip` | pill badges | Status chips, cadence labels |

**New components introduced by Phase 8:**
1. **Secondary MetricCard** вҖ” Same as MetricCard but with smaller text (xl instead of 2xl) and `lr-card` (not `lr-card-strong`). No new component needed вҖ” just prop-driven sizing.
2. **BoxUpdateCard** вҖ” For displaying box contents on store page. Uses `lr-card-strong` with optional image left, text right.
3. **StatusChip** вҖ” Already exists as inline styles in the payout table. Should be formalized with 4 variants: `transferred` (leaf), `processing` (amber), `retrying` (clay), `action-needed` (clay + bold).

No new design tokens needed. All Phase 8 UI should use the existing vocabulary.

**Pass 5 re-rated: 5/10 вҶ’ 8/10** (explicit token mapping added, no new tokens needed).

---

## Pass 6: Responsive & Accessibility

**Rating: 1/10** вҖ” Zero responsive or accessibility specs in the plan.

**Responsive behavior per new surface:**

| Surface | Desktop | Tablet | Mobile |
|---------|---------|--------|--------|
| Analytics primary row | 4 cols (`grid-cols-4`) | 2 cols (`md:grid-cols-4` вҶ’ `grid-cols-2`) | 2 cols (existing pattern) |
| Analytics secondary row | 3 cols (`grid-cols-3`) | 3 cols | Stack to 1 col on narrow mobile |
| Top products list | Full width below metrics | Same | Same |
| Box update card (buyer) | Image left, text right | Same | Image on top, text below (stack) |
| City landing page grid | 3 cols (existing `lg:grid-cols-3`) | 2 cols (`sm:grid-cols-2`) | 1 col (stack) |
| Location picker (subscribe form) | Radio buttons inline | Same | Stack vertically |
| Seller box update form | Full-width form in dashboard | Same | Same |

**Accessibility specs:**

| Element | A11y Requirement |
|---------|-----------------|
| Star rating display | `aria-label="4.8 out of 5 stars"` вҖ” screen reader gets the number, not decorative stars |
| Trend arrows (в–І/в–ј) | `aria-label="Up 3 from last period"` вҖ” arrow is decorative, label carries meaning |
| Payout status chips | Already use text labels (good) вҖ” ensure color is not the only indicator |
| Location picker radio buttons | Standard radio group with `role="radiogroup"`, `aria-labelledby` |
| Waitlist form | `aria-live="polite"` on success message so screen readers announce it |
| City landing page | `<h1>` for city name, `<main>` landmark, breadcrumb with `aria-label="Breadcrumb"` |
| Box update card | Image has `alt` text from store name, or `alt=""` if decorative |
| Touch targets | All buttons вүҘ 44px (existing `py-2` buttons are ~40px вҖ” should verify during implementation) |

## Pass 7: Unresolved Design Decisions

Surfacing ambiguities that would haunt implementation:

```
DECISION NEEDED                              | IF DEFERRED, WHAT HAPPENS
---------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------
1. How does seller post a box update?         | Engineer invents a form, no spec
2. OG image layout specifics                  | Engineer picks a gradient template
3. Star rating rendering (filled/outline?)    | Engineer uses emoji or a random lib
4. Subscriber trend period (vs what?)         | "в–І +3" compared to... last week?
```

Let me resolve these one by one.

**Decision 1: Box update posting form**

The seller taps "Post an update" near their upcoming pickup on the dashboard. This opens:

```

Uses existing `ImageUpload` component (already in seller settings). Title is a single line, body is a textarea. Simple form вҖ” no rich text, no markdown.

**Decision 2:** OG image uses `--lr-bg` sand background, `--lr-ink` text, `--lr-leaf` accent bar on left. Store name (large), price range, review stars if available. Uses Next.js `ImageResponse` API вҖ” no external service. Fallback when no hero image: just text on sand background.

**Decision 3:** Star rating will be rendered as 5 SVG stars вҖ” filled with `--lr-leaf` for whole stars, half-filled for halves, outline for empty. Same pattern used in the existing `ReviewSummary` component on the store detail page (which already renders stars). Reuse that component.

FROM order_items oi
JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id
WHERE o.store_id = $1 AND o.status = 'picked_up'
GROUP BY oi.product_title
ORDER BY total_qty DESC
LIMIT 5;
```

# Phase 8: Growth & Retention вҖ” Implementation Plan

**Goal:** Transform Local Roots from a functional marketplace into a growth-ready platform with buyer retention loops, seller engagement analytics, organic acquisition via SEO, and hardened payout infrastructure.

**North Star:** Increase completed pickups per week by building the systems that keep buyers coming back and help sellers see the value of the platform.

**Architecture:** Go backend (new endpoints + cron jobs), Next.js App Router frontend (new pages + enhanced components), PostgreSQL (new queries, no schema changes expected), Resend (new email templates).

---

**Architecture:** Go backend (new endpoints + cron jobs), Next.js App Router frontend (new pages + enhanced components), PostgreSQL (new queries, no schema changes expected), Resend (new email templates).

---

## Design Specifications (added by design review)

### Design Tokens (from globals.css вҖ” no new tokens needed)
- `--lr-bg` (#f6f1e8 sand) вҖ” page backgrounds, OG image background
- `--lr-ink` (#1c1b16 ink) вҖ” primary text, metric values
- `--lr-muted` (#4a463c peat) вҖ” labels, secondary text, timestamps
- `--lr-leaf` (#2f6b4f sage) вҖ” positive indicators, CTAs, filled stars, trend в–І
- `--lr-clay` (#b35d2e clay) вҖ” warning/error indicators, trend в–ј, "Action needed"
- `lr-card` / `lr-card-strong` вҖ” standard (78%) / emphasized (92%) card opacity
- `lr-chip` вҖ” pill badges for status and cadence

### A11y: Bump `lr-btn-primary` touch targets to `py-2.5` (44px min) in globals.css.

### Analytics Page вҖ” Two-Tier Metric Layout
```
PRIMARY ROW (4 cards, lr-card-strong, 2xl values):
  Active Subs (в–І/в–ј delta vs 4 weeks ago) | Pickup Rate | Revenue (в–І/в–ј growth %) | Avg Rating (вҳ… stars)

SECONDARY ROW (3 cards, lr-card, xl values, muted):
  Retention Rate | No-Show Rate | Churn

TOP PRODUCTS (ranked list section, not chart):
  1. Product title вҖ” qty sold вҖ” revenue

EXISTING SECTIONS (unchanged): Revenue by Cycle table, Payout History table
```
- Subscriber trend: в–І/в–ј arrow with delta vs 4 weeks ago on Active Subs card
- Avg Rating: reuse ReviewSummary star component, show "No reviews yet" when null
- All trend arrows: `aria-label="Up 3 from 4 weeks ago"` for screen readers
- Empty state (new store): "Your store is brand new вҖ” metrics appear after your first pickup"

### "What's in the Box" вҖ” Buyer Display
- Placed on store detail page **above** "What's available" section
- Single `lr-card-strong` with optional photo left (200px), title + body right
- "Posted X days ago" timestamp in `--lr-muted`
- If no box update exists: section doesn't render (not an empty state)
- Mobile: photo stacks on top, text below
- Photo `alt`: store name + "box contents"

### "What's in the Box" вҖ” Seller Posting UI
- Inline on seller dashboard, near upcoming pickup (not in settings)
- Empty prompt: "Share what's in this week's box with your subscribers"
- Form: Title (single line), Body (textarea), Photo (optional, reuse `ImageUpload`)
- Post/Cancel buttons, loading state "PostingвҖҰ" with disabled button
- Success: toast "Update posted!" + card appears
- Error: `ErrorAlert` with retry

### City Landing Page (`/farms/{city-region}`)
- h1: "Farm Boxes in {City}, {Region}"
- Subtitle: "Fresh local food, picked up weekly from farms near you."
- Reuse existing `StoreCard` in `sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3` grid
- Empty state: warm copy + waitlist email capture (reuse stores page pattern)
- Breadcrumb: Farms вҶ’ {City}, {Region}

### Waitlist Form (stores page)
- Already partially built (notifyEmail/notifySubmitted state exists)
- Enhancement: submit geo coords (lat/lng from search context) to `POST /v1/waitlist`
- Success: "You're on the list!" with green checkmark (existing pattern)
- Duplicate: "You're already on our list"

### Payout Status Chips (4 states)
- Transferred: `--lr-leaf` bg/10, leaf text (existing)
- Processing: amber bg, amber text (new)
- Retrying: `--lr-clay` bg/10, clay text (new)
- Action needed: `--lr-clay` bg/10, clay text, bold (new)

### OG Image (`/stores/{storeId}/opengraph-image`)
- Background: `--lr-bg` sand
- Left accent bar: `--lr-leaf` sage (4px)
- Text: `--lr-ink` вҖ” store name (large), price range, review stars if available
- No gradients, no stock photos вҖ” branded fallback when no hero image
- Built with Next.js `ImageResponse` API

### Responsive Breakpoints
| Surface | Desktop | Tablet | Mobile |
|---------|---------|--------|--------|
| Analytics primary row | `md:grid-cols-4` | `grid-cols-2` | `grid-cols-2` |
| Analytics secondary row | `grid-cols-3` | `grid-cols-3` | `grid-cols-1` |
| Box update card (buyer) | Image left, text right | Same | Image top, text bottom |
| City landing grid | `lg:grid-cols-3` | `sm:grid-cols-2` | `grid-cols-1` |
| Location picker | Radio inline | Same | Stack vertical |

### Interaction State Coverage
| Feature | Loading | Empty | Error | Success |
|---------|---------|-------|-------|---------|
| Analytics (enhanced) | "Loading analyticsвҖҰ" | "Brand new вҖ” metrics after first pickup" | Toast | Two-tier cards |
| Box update (buyer) | N/A (SSR) | Section hidden | Graceful degradation | Card renders |
| Box update (seller) | "PostingвҖҰ" disabled | "Share what's in this week's box" | ErrorAlert | Toast + card |
| Waitlist capture | "JoiningвҖҰ" disabled | N/A (shows on 0 results) | Inline error | "You're on the list!" |
| City page | Skeleton cards (3) | Warm copy + waitlist | "Couldn't load" + retry | Store grid |
| Top products | Part of page load | "No orders yet" | Part of analytics | Ranked list |
| Payout status | Existing table | Existing | Existing | 4-state chips |
| Multi-location mgmt | "SavingвҖҰ" | "Add first pickup location" | ErrorAlert | Toast |
